summary.intent: Reduce the overwhelming model list in the LLM settings UI (111+ models for OpenAI alone) by collapsing lists, surfacing preferred/recommended models first, and hiding legacy models from the chat selector.
summary.outcome: Implemented collapsible model lists (top 3 visible by default), added a `recommended` field throughout the Rust provider stack, pattern-matched OpenAI flagship models, marked static catalog flagships, added legacy model filtering (>1 year old) to the chat model selector with a config override, and updated Anthropic's catalog to include the 4.6 model family.

repo: ModelInfo is constructed in many places across providers/src/lib.rs, openai.rs, openai_codex.rs, github_copilot.rs, local_llm_setup.rs, swift-bridge/src/lib.rs — adding a new field requires touching all of them
Static model catalogs use catalog_to_discovered() helper; the first N entries get recommended=true based on position in the array
models.list() had `preferred` field but models.list_all() (used by settings page) did not — they diverged silently
The sessions table stores a `model TEXT` column that could be used for real usage-based popularity in the future
providers.show_legacy_models config field follows the is_false skip_serializing_if pattern used elsewhere in config/src/schema.rs
Anthropic catalog needed updating: correct flagship models are now Opus 4.6, Sonnet 4.6, Haiku 4.6 (not 4.5)

code.finding: Anthropic catalog uses destructuring pattern (model_id, display_name, created_at) — adding recommended required updating both destructuring and ModelInfo construction
parse_model_entry() is where OpenAI API-discovered models get created — flagship detection by pattern-matching known model IDs without date suffixes happens here
Legacy model filter uses SystemTime; models without created_at (static catalog entries) and preferred models are exempt from the 1-year cutoff
Bulk sed script to add recommended: false incorrectly targeted PendingInvoke struct fields — needed manual cleanup
Settings page sort order: preferred > recommended > most recent; collapses to 3 models by default with toggle

workflow: Bulk sed to add new struct fields across a large codebase is risky — it matched non-target structs (PendingInvoke) and struct definitions in swift-bridge, requiring manual fixes
Compiling after each batch of struct field additions and fixing errors iteratively is more reliable than trying to find all sites upfront
is_false helper for skip_serializing_if must be defined if not already present — check before referencing

friction: Adding recommended field to ModelInfo required touching 15+ construction sites across 6+ files — no struct update or builder pattern to reduce blast radius
Bulk sed script incorrectly modified non-ModelInfo structs (PendingInvoke in server.rs, struct definition in swift-bridge) requiring manual cleanup
models.list() and models.list_all() had diverged — preferred was missing from list_all, discovered only mid-implementation
Test timestamps used old epoch values (1600000000 = Sept 2020) that triggered the new legacy filter, causing test failures
open_items: Popularity before first selection: add a `recommended` field to onboarding model selector based on provider-curated tiers — not yet implemented for onboarding view
Real usage-based popularity: sessions table has model column, could add RPC `SELECT model, COUNT(*) FROM sessions GROUP BY model` to sort by actual user usage
OpenAI-compatible providers (MiniMax, Z.AI, Moonshot, Ollama) only get top-2 recommended by position — no flagship pattern matching like OpenAI has
Reasoning variants (@reasoning-low/medium/high) are explicitly excluded from recommended — may want to reconsider for power users
GitHub Copilot and OpenAI Codex flagships are marked by catalog position only, not by model ID pattern matching
